title: Admin CRUD endpoints for users
description: Implement `users.list`, `users.create`, `users.update` (role/email), and `users.deactivate` (or delete) tRPC mutations, all gated by `adminProcedure`.
acceptanceCriteria: `users.list` (adminProcedure query) returns all users with `id, email, role` — never `senha_hash`
`users.create` (adminProcedure mutation) accepts `{ email, password, role }`, hashes the password via TASK-9's `hashPassword`, creates the `users` and `memberships` rows
`users.create` with a duplicate email returns a `CONFLICT`/`BAD_REQUEST` error, not a raw database error
`users.deactivate` (adminProcedure mutation) prevents the acting admin from deactivating their own account
All mutations reject non-admin callers with `FORBIDDEN` (enforced by `adminProcedure`)

steps.description: Create users router with list query
steps.details: Create `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/users.ts`. Add `list: adminProcedure.query(...)` selecting `id, email` from `users` joined with `memberships.role`, excluding `senha_hash`.

Add `create: adminProcedure.input(zod schema).mutation(...)` that calls `hashPassword`, inserts into `users`, then inserts a `memberships` row with the given role. Wrap the unique-email insert in a try/catch and rethrow as `TRPCError({ code: 'CONFLICT' })` on constraint violation.
Add `update: adminProcedure.input(z.object({ userId: z.string(), role: z.enum(['admin','editor']) })).mutation(...)` updating the `memberships.role` row for the target user.

Add `deactivate: adminProcedure.input(z.object({ userId: z.string() })).mutation(...)`. If `input.userId === ctx.user.userId`, throw `TRPCError({ code: 'BAD_REQUEST', message: 'Cannot deactivate your own account' })`. Otherwise remove/disable the user (e.g. delete the row or add an `ativo` flag if soft-delete is preferred).

Test as admin: list returns users without password hash; create succeeds and rejects duplicate email; update changes role; deactivate blocks self-deactivation and succeeds for other users. Test as editor: every procedure returns FORBIDDEN.

technicalNotes: Decide and document whether `deactivate` is a hard delete or soft-delete flag before implementing — a hard delete simplifies the MVP but orphans `revisions.autor_id` references; consider keeping the user row and clearing credentials/active flag instead.
